feed.title: Jiachuan Wang
feed.subtitle: Reality is merely an illusion, albeit a very persistent one.

entry.title: About Me
entry.summary:
Hello! My name is Wang Jiachuan, Elmer. I am currently pursuing a Ph.D. at the National University of Singapore under the guidance of Andrew Tan, Camilo Libedinsky, and Shih-Cheng Yen. I hold dual B.Sc. degrees in Biomedical Informatics from Zhejiang University and the University of Edinburgh, where I completed my thesis on multi-voxel pattern analysis of human memory and emotions under the supervision of Gedi Lukšys. I also conducted a research training project under Anna Wang Roe, where I contributed to experiments, data analysis, and visualization of Infrared Neural Stimulation (INS) with fMRI.

My research interests center on the neurocomputational mechanisms underlying cognition, ranging from decoding fMRI data using machine learning techniques to modeling learning and memory in behavioral experiments.

Recently, I am focused on two projects: 1) modeling spatial learning and associative memory using biologically plausible rules, and 2) investigating the basis of freezing behavior through models of plasticity.

Selected Publications

  • Kong, X., Shu, X., Wang, J., Liu, D., Ni, Y., Zhao, W., Wang, L., Gao, Z., Chen, J., Yang, B., Guo, X. and Wang, Z. (2022) Fine-tuning of mTOR signaling by the UBE4B-KLHL22 E3 ubiquitin ligase cascade in brain development., Development, 149(24). doi: 10.1242/dev.201286. PubMed

You can learn more about me here.
entry.content:
Hello! My name is Wang Jiachuan, Elmer. I am currently pursuing a Ph.D. at the National University of Singapore under the guidance of Andrew Tan, Camilo Libedinsky, and Shih-Cheng Yen. I hold dual B.Sc. degrees in Biomedical Informatics from Zhejiang University and the University of Edinburgh, where I completed my thesis on multi-voxel pattern analysis of human memory and emotions under the supervision of Gedi Lukšys. I also conducted a research training project under Anna Wang Roe, where I contributed to experiments, data analysis, and visualization of Infrared Neural Stimulation (INS) with fMRI.

My research interests center on the neurocomputational mechanisms underlying cognition, ranging from decoding fMRI data using machine learning techniques to modeling learning and memory in behavioral experiments.

Recently, I am focused on two projects: 1) modeling spatial learning and associative memory using biologically plausible rules, and 2) investigating the basis of freezing behavior through models of plasticity.

Selected Publications

  • Kong, X., Shu, X., Wang, J., Liu, D., Ni, Y., Zhao, W., Wang, L., Gao, Z., Chen, J., Yang, B., Guo, X. and Wang, Z. (2022) Fine-tuning of mTOR signaling by the UBE4B-KLHL22 E3 ubiquitin ligase cascade in brain development., Development, 149(24). doi: 10.1242/dev.201286. PubMed

You can learn more about me here.

entry.title: LTR Predictor: HERV-oriented Alignment Tool
entry.summary: The top-5 mini-projects of Biomedical Informatics 3 (BMI3) coursework. Link here: https://labw.org/bmi3-2021/miniprojects .
entry.content: The top-5 mini-projects of Biomedical Informatics 3 (BMI3) coursework. Link here: https://labw.org/bmi3-2021/miniprojects .
